title: Navigation Mechanism from Approved Requirement to Plan Configuration and Rule Builder
description: The system shall provide navigation actions from an approved requirement to applicable Plan Configuration and Rule Builder components. The Requirements Studio shall expose [Create Rule] and [Apply to Plan] actions on each approved requirement.

architectureDecision.principle: Approved AI-generated requirements shall be converted into structured domain objects and made available through a shared Requirements API. Users shall explicitly select 'Apply to Plan Configuration' or 'Create Rule'. Raw LLM output shall never directly modify published plan configuration.
architectureDecision.flow: Documents → AI Extraction → Requirements Studio → Human Review → Approve Requirement → Normalize → [Create Rule | Apply to Plan] → Draft → Human Review → Publish

apiDesign.purpose: Generate rule draft pre-populated from requirement
Apply requirement to selected plan draft
Return impacted plans, rules, and artifacts
Return source and downstream traceability relationships

title: Canonical Requirement Representation Independent of AI Model and UI
description: Approved requirements shall be stored in a canonical, structured representation independent of the AI model and UI implementation. The canonical requirement is the bridge between Requirements Studio, Rule Builder, and Plan Configuration.

title: Requirement Type to Downstream Configuration Target Mapping
description: The system shall map requirement types to appropriate downstream configuration targets. Not every requirement type applies to every screen.

title: Create Draft Business Rule from Approved Eligibility or Business-Rule Requirement
description: Users shall be able to create a draft business rule from an approved eligibility or business-rule requirement using the [Create Rule] action in Requirements Studio.

title: Rule Builder Pre-Populated from Approved Requirement Conditions and Actions
description: The Rule Builder shall pre-populate rule conditions and actions from the approved requirement when opened via [Create Rule]. A banner shall indicate the AI source requirement, document citation, and confidence.

title: Apply Approved Requirement to Selected Plan Configuration
description: Users shall be able to apply an approved requirement to a selected plan configuration using the [Apply to Plan] action. A modal shall show the target plan dropdown and a list of configuration fields affected.

title: Preview Proposed Plan Configuration Changes Before Applying
description: The system shall display a preview of proposed plan configuration changes before applying them. The preview shall show the field, current value, proposed value, source requirement, and AI confidence as a diff.

title: AI-Generated Changes Applied to Plan Draft — Never Directly to Published Plan
description: AI-generated changes shall be applied to a plan draft and shall not directly modify a published plan. The user must explicitly review and publish the draft for changes to take effect.
safetyPrinciple: Raw AI output shall never directly modify production benefits configuration. Human review of the plan draft is always required before publication.

title: Bidirectional Traceability: Source Document → Requirement → Rule → Plan Configuration
description: The system shall maintain traceability between source document, requirement, rule, and plan configuration. The traceability graph shall be navigable — clicking any node navigates to that object.

title: Bidirectional Navigation Between Requirement and Its Downstream Rules and Plan Configurations
description: The system shall provide bidirectional navigation between a requirement and its downstream rules and plan configurations. From a rule or plan configuration, users shall be able to navigate back to the originating requirement.

title: Impact Detection When an Approved Requirement Is Modified
description: The system shall identify downstream artifacts affected when an approved requirement is modified. Impact detection shall cover rules, plans, and test cases. Users shall see an impact warning before confirming the modification.

title: Only Approved Requirements Eligible for Downstream Application
description: Only requirements in APPROVED status shall be eligible for downstream application to plan configuration or published business rules. Requirements in AI_EXTRACTED, UNDER_REVIEW, or REJECTED status shall not be applicable.
